heading: Example
body:
The following index definition example indexes the `text.en_US`
field in the [sample collection](custom-analyzers-eg-coll) collection
using a custom analyzer named `htmlStrippingAnalyzer`. The
custom analyzer specifies the following:

- Remove all HTML tags from the text except the `a` tag using the
  `htmlStrip` character filter.

- Generate tokens based on word break rules from the [Unicode Text
  Segmentation algorithm](https://www.unicode.org/L2/L2019/19034-uax29-34-draft.pdf)
  using the [standard](standard-tokenizer-ref) tokenizer.

Atlas UI

Visual Editor

- In the Custom Analyzers section, click Add
  Custom Analyzer.

- Select the Create Your Own radio button and click
  Next.

- Type whitespace in the Analyzer Name field.

- Expand Character Filters and click
   Add character filter.

- Select mapping from the dropdown and type
  a in the ignoredTags
  field.

- Click Add character filter.

- Expand Tokenizer if it's collapsed and select
  whitespace from the dropdown.

- Click Add to add the custom analyzer to your index.

- In the Field Mappings section, click Add
  Field Mapping to apply the custom analyzer on the
  message field.

- Select message from the Field
  Name dropdown and dateFacet from the Data
  Type dropdown.

- In the properties section for the data type, select whitespace
  from the Index Analyzer and Search Analyzer
  dropdowns.

- Click Add, then Save Changes.

JSON Editor

Replace the default index definition with the following:

```json
 {
   "mappings": {
     "fields": {
       "text": {
         "type": "document",
         "dynamic": true,
         "fields": {
           "en_US": {
             "analyzer": "htmlStrippingAnalyzer",
             "type": "string"
           }
         }
       }
     }
   },
   "analyzers": [{
     "name": "htmlStrippingAnalyzer",
     "charFilters": [{
       "type": "htmlStrip",
       "ignoredTags": ["a"]
     }],
     "tokenizer": {
       "type": "standard"
     },
     "tokenFilters": []
   }]
 }
```

MongoDB Shell

The following query looks for occurrences of the string `head` in
the `text.en_US` field of the `minutes` collection.

Atlas UI

MongoDB Shell

MongoDB Search doesn't return the document with `_id: 1` because the
string `head` is part of the HTML tag `<head>`. The
document with `_id: 3` contains HTML tags, but the string
`head` is elsewhere so the document is a match. The following
table shows the tokens that MongoDB Search generates for the `text.en_US`
field values in documents `_id: 1`, `_id: 2`, and  `_id: 3` in
the [sample collection](custom-analyzers-eg-coll) collection using the
`htmlStrippingAnalyzer`.

| Document ID | Output Tokens                                         |
| ----------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
| \_id: 1     | This, page, deals, with, department, meetings         |
| \_id: 2     | The, head, of, the, sales, department, spoke, first   |
| \_id: 3     | We'll, head, out, to, the, conference, room, by, noon |
